title: foster_city_secondary_2025 (window 0)
text:
ORDINANCE NO. 696 
 
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY OF FOSTER CITY AMENDING SECTION 1.01.010, 
“ADOPTION-SECONDARY CODES ADOPTED,” OF CHAPTER 1.01, “CODE 
ADOPTION,” WITHIN TITLE 1 OF THE FOSTER CITY MUNICIPAL CODE ADOPTING 
BY REFERENCE CERTAIN PARTS, INCLUDING RELATED APPENDICES, AS 
IDENTIFIED IN THE 2025 CALIFORNIA BUILDING CODE, 2025 RESIDENTIAL CODE, 
2025 CALIFORNIA MECHANICAL CODE, 2025 CALIFORNIA PLUMBING CODE, 2025 
CALIFORNIA ELECTRICAL CODE, 2025 CALIFORNIA FIRE CODE, 2025 CALIFORNIA 
ENERGY CODE, 2025 CALIFORNIA GREEN BUILDING STANDARDS CODE,  THE 
2025 CALIFORNIA EXISTING BUILDING CODE AND THE 2025 WILDLAND-URBAN 
INTERFACE CODE WITHIN TITLE 24 OF THE CALIFORNIA CODE OF REGULATIONS; 
AND 
FINDING 
THE 
AMENDMENTS 
EXEMPT 
FROM 
THE 
CALIFORNIA 
ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY ACT (CEQA) PURSUANT TO CEQA GUIDELINES 
SECTION 15061(B)(3) 
 
CITY OF FOSTER CITY 
 
 
THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF FOSTER CITY DOES HEREBY ORDAIN, as 
follows: 
 
Section 1. The City Council finds that with the adoption of the various codes and 
amendments found in Title 15, namely the California Building Code, California Residential 
Code, California Plumbing Code, California Mechanical Code, California Electrical Code, 
California Fire Code, California Energy Code, California Existing Building Code, the 
California Green Building Standards Code and the California Wildland-Urban Interface 
Code, is necessary to adopt an ordinance to update Title 1, Chapter 1, Section 1.01.010 
to 
reflect 
the 
current 
edition 
of 
the 
California 
Building 
Standards 
. 
 
Section 2.  Amendment. Section 1.01.010, “Adoption-Secondary codes adopted” of 
Chapter 1.01 within Title 1, “General Provisions” is hereby amended by repealing and 
replacing in its entirety to read as follows: 
 
“1.01.010 Adoption -- Secondary codes adopted. 
Pursuant to the provisions of Sections 50022.1 through 50022.10 of the Government 
Code of the state of California, there is adopted the “Foster City Code,” including the 
follo

title: foster_city_secondary_2025 (window 1)
text:
ondary codes adopted. 
Pursuant to the provisions of Sections 50022.1 through 50022.10 of the Government 
Code of the state of California, there is adopted the “Foster City Code,” including the 
following secondary codes incorporated by reference in whole or in part, including related 
appendices, as identified in Title 24 of the California Code of Regulations, Parts 2, (Vols. 
1 
&

A.

International Code Council, with California Amendments; 
B.

International Code Council, with California Amendments;

C.

International Association of Plumbing and Mechanical Officials, with California 
Amendments; 
D. 
Code entitled “California Plumbing Code”, 2025 Edition, published by the 
International Association of Plumbing and Mechanical Officials, with California 
Amendments; 
E.

Fire Protection Association, with California Amendments; 
F. 
Code entitled “California Fire Code”, 2025 Edition, published by the International 
Code Council, with California Amendments; 
G. 
Code entitled “California Energy Code”, 2025 Edition, published by the 
International Code Council, with California Amendments; 
H. 
Code entitled “California Green Building Standards Code”, 2025 Edition, published 
by the International Code Council; 
I. 
Code entitled “California Existing Building Code”, 2025 Edition, published by the 
International Code Council; 
J. 
Code entitled “California Wildland-Urban Interface Code, 2025 Edition., published 
by the International Code Council. 
 
Save and except those portions of the preceding secondary codes as are deleted,

title: foster_city_secondary_2025 (window 2)
text:
ode entitled “California Wildland-Urban Interface Code, 2025 Edition., published 
by the International Code Council. 
 
Save and except those portions of the preceding secondary codes as are deleted, 
modified or amended by provisions of the Foster City Code. 
 
From and after the effective date of the ordinances codified in this chapter, the Foster City 
Code shall constitute the penal and regulatory ordinances of the city of Foster City.” 
 
Section 3. CEQA. The adoption of this ordinance is exempt from the California 
Environmental Quality Act (“CEQA”), in that the adoption of State codes and the local 
amendments herein described do not have the potential for causing a significant effect 
on the environment, pursuant to Sections 15061(b) (3) and 15378(b) (5) of the CEQA 
Guidelines (Title 14, Chapter 3 of the California Code of Regulations). The adoption of 
this ordinance is also exempt from CEQA pursuant to Section 15308 of the CEQA 
Guidelines as actions by a regulatory agency to assure the maintenance, restoration, 
enhancement, or protection of the environment 
 
Section 4. Repeal of Conflicting Ordinances. All former Ordinances or parts thereof 
conflicting or inconsistent with the provisions of this Ordinance or of the Code hereby 
adopted are thereby repealed. 
 
Section 5. Severability. If any section, subsection, sentence, clause, or phrase of the 
Ordinance is for any reason held to be invalid, such decision shall not affect the validity 
of the remaining portions of this Ordinance. The City Council hereby declares that it would 
have adopted the Ordinance and each section, subsection, clause, or phrase thereof, 
irrespective of the fact that any one or more sections, subsections, sentences, clauses, 
or phrases be declared invalid. 
 
Section 6.  Taking Effect. This Ordinance shall take effect on January 1, 2026. 
Docusign Envelope ID: 625F0212-95E1-4F5E-B77C-D18BDEFE3A85

Effect. This Ordinance shall take effect on January 1, 2026.

City Ordinance No. 696 
 
 
Section 7. Posting. Within 15 days after the passage of this Ordinance, the City Clerk 
shall have it posted in three public places designated by resolution of the City Council. 
 
This Ordinance was introduced and read on the 3rd day of November, 2025, and 
passed and adopted on the 17th day of November, 2025, by the following vote: 
 
AYES: Councilmembers Kiesel, Niederhofer, Sullivan, Venkat, and Mayor Jimenez 
 
 
NOES: None 
 
 
 
ABSENT: None 
 
 
 
 
ABSTAIN: None 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
       
  
STACY JIMENEZ, MAYOR 
 
 
ATTEST: 
 
 
 
PRISCILLA SCHAUS, CITY CLERK
